title: Normal Accidents
author: Charles Perrow
content: “Complex, tightly-coupled systems are prone to inevitable, unpredictable failure.”
action: Accept that failure is an inherent feature of your high-complexity environments.

title: The Challenger Launch Decision
author: Diane Vaughan
content: “The normalization of deviance occurs when minor failures are accepted as routine until they lead to catastrophe.”
action: Identify and arrest the 'drift' from safety standards before catastrophe occurs.

title: To Engineer is Human
author: Henry Petroski
content: “Success is the worst teacher; failure provides the forensic data necessary for true innovation.”
action: Examine your system's rupture points to define its design limits.

title: Chernobyl: The History
author: Serhii Plokhy
content: “The disaster was a result of a design flaw covered up by a culture of absolute institutional secrecy.”
action: Eliminate institutional secrecy to prevent localized failures from scaling.

title: Too Big to Fail
author: Andrew Ross Sorkin
content: “The systemic risk of the 2008 collapse lay in the interconnectedness of institutions that were all betting on the same flawed model.”
action: Diversify your underlying assumptions to avoid systemic bankruptcy.
